comments:
Group — Hierarchical grouping for Schema entities.

## Purpose

At billion-agent scale, entities need to be organized into hierarchies:
- Color palettes with semantic groupings (primary, secondary, accent)
- Typography scales (heading, body, caption)
- Component libraries (atoms, molecules, compounds)

Groups provide:
1. **Hierarchical organization** — Parent-child relationships
2. **Named collections** — Human/agent-readable labels
3. **Ordered members** — Deterministic traversal order
4. **UUID5 identity** — Content-addressed group IDs

## Design

Groups form a forest (multiple roots allowed). Each group:
- Has a unique ID (deterministic from path)
- Has a name (human-readable)
- Contains ordered items
- May have a parent group

## Invariants

1. **Acyclic**: A group cannot be its own ancestor
2. **Unique paths**: Each path through the hierarchy is unique
3. **Determinism**: Same structure → same UUIDs

## Usage

```purescript
-- Create a color palette hierarchy
let palette = groupWithChildren "colors" "Brand Colors"
      [ leaf "primary" "Primary" [swatch1, swatch2]
      , leaf "secondary" "Secondary" [swatch3]
      , groupWithChildren "accents" "Accents"
          [ leaf "warm" "Warm" [orange, red]
          , leaf "cool" "Cool" [blue, teal]
          ]
      ]
```

declarations.comments:
A hierarchical group of items.

Groups can contain:
- Ordered items of type `a`
- Child groups (forming a tree)

The group's ID is deterministically derived from its path.
declarations.title: Group

declarations.comments:
Deterministic identifier for a group.

Generated from the group's path in the hierarchy.
Same path → same GroupId (always).
declarations.title: GroupId

declarations.comments: The internal record structure of a group.
declarations.title: GroupRecord

declarations.comments: Create a group with a name (convenience for creating branch groups)
declarations.title: mkGroup

declarations.comments: Create a leaf group (no children, has items)
declarations.title: leaf

declarations.comments: Create a group with children (automatically sets parent references)
declarations.title: groupWithChildren

declarations.comments:
Create an empty group with a name

The slug is used for path construction.
The ID is derived from the slug path.
declarations.title: emptyGroup

declarations.comments: Get the group's ID
declarations.title: groupId

declarations.comments: Get the group's name
declarations.title: groupName

declarations.comments: Get the group's slug
declarations.title: groupSlug

declarations.comments: Get the group's items
declarations.title: groupItems

declarations.comments: Get the group's children
declarations.title: groupChildren

declarations.comments: Get the group's parent ID
declarations.title: groupParent

declarations.comments: Unwrap a group to access its record
declarations.title: unwrapGroup

declarations.comments: Add an item to a group
declarations.title: addItem

declarations.comments: Add multiple items to a group
declarations.title: addItems

declarations.comments: Remove an item from a group (by equality)
declarations.title: removeItem

declarations.comments: Add a child group
declarations.title: addChild

declarations.comments: Remove a child group by ID
declarations.title: removeChild

declarations.comments: Set the group's name
declarations.title: setGroupName

declarations.comments: Set the group's parent (for tree restructuring)
declarations.title: setParent

declarations.comments: Get all items in a group and its descendants
declarations.title: allItems

declarations.comments: Get all groups in the tree (this group and all descendants)
declarations.title: allGroups

declarations.comments:
Get the ancestor chain (parent, grandparent, ..., root)

Requires the full forest to traverse upward.
declarations.title: ancestors

declarations.comments: Get all descendant groups
declarations.title: descendants

declarations.comments: Get the depth of a group in the hierarchy (0 = root)
declarations.title: groupDepth

declarations.comments: Get the path from root to this group
declarations.title: groupPath

declarations.comments: Get the path as a string (slug/slug/slug)
declarations.title: pathString

declarations.comments: Check if a group has no items
declarations.title: isEmpty

declarations.comments: Check if a group is a leaf (no children)
declarations.title: isLeaf

declarations.comments: Check if a group is a root (no parent)
declarations.title: isRoot

declarations.comments: Check if a group contains an item
declarations.title: hasItem

declarations.comments: Find a group by ID in a tree
declarations.title: findGroup

declarations.comments: Find an item by predicate in a group and its descendants
declarations.title: findItem

declarations.comments:
A forest is a collection of root groups.

Multiple independent trees can exist in a forest.
declarations.title: Forest

declarations.comments: Empty forest
declarations.title: emptyForest

declarations.comments: Add a root group to the forest
declarations.title: addRoot

declarations.comments: Remove a root group from the forest
declarations.title: removeRoot

declarations.comments: Get all root groups in the forest
declarations.title: forestRoots

declarations.comments: Get all items in the entire forest
declarations.title: forestAllItems

declarations.comments: Get all groups in the entire forest
declarations.title: forestAllGroups

declarations.comments: Find a group by ID anywhere in the forest
declarations.title: findGroupInForest

declarations.comments: Unwrap GroupId to string
declarations.title: unwrapGroupId

declarations.comments: Create GroupId from path components
declarations.title: makeGroupId

name: Hydrogen.Schema.Group
